purpose: Scholarship research for Avery - Pre-vet/Animal Science track, Georgia resident, 3.85 GPA, 27 ACT, Class of 2027
notes: Deadlines listed are typical cycle dates. For the 2026-2027 application cycle (Fall 2027 enrollment), confirm exact dates on each organization's website as they may shift slightly year to year.

scholarships.name: APVMA Community Service Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Pre-veterinary students who demonstrate commitment to animal welfare, animal care, or public health through volunteer work, internships, or service
scholarships.notes: Must be an APVMA member. Summer 2026 scholarships now open — deadline July 1, 2026.

scholarships.name: APVMA Resiliency Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Pre-vet students or APVMA club members who have demonstrated resilience and need financial assistance
scholarships.notes: Requires APVMA membership

scholarships.name: APVMA One Health Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Pre-vet students committed to interconnected health of people, animals, and the environment; must demonstrate leadership
scholarships.notes: Requires APVMA membership

scholarships.name: Vince's Pre-Veterinary Student Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Undergraduate students enrolled in pre-veterinary or animal science program in the U.S. with minimum 3.33 GPA. Must write a poem (max 500 words) about animals.
scholarships.notes: Avery qualifies with 3.85 GPA. Apply once enrolled as a freshman.

scholarships.name: National Dairy Shrine / Maurice E. Core Freshman Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: College freshmen majoring in dairy/animal industry related field with interest in dairy industry. Based on leadership, volunteerism, activities, and future plans.
scholarships.notes: Apply once enrolled as a college freshman. Applications open March 1.

scholarships.name: National Dairy Shrine Pre-Vet Scholarship (Zoetis-sponsored)
scholarships.eligibility: Student enrolled in a pre-vet program in their 3rd or 4th year of undergraduate study
scholarships.notes: For later in college career, not freshman year. Worth bookmarking for junior/senior year.

scholarships.name: Charles Shelton Veterinary Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Black/African-American high school seniors and college students pursuing veterinary careers. Minimum 3.0 GPA.
scholarships.notes: Only applicable if Avery meets the demographic eligibility requirement.

scholarships.name: AKC Humane Fund Sir John D. Spurling Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Full-time students enrolled in degree or certification program at accredited U.S. institution focusing on care of pets. Requires 2 letters of recommendation and transcript.
scholarships.notes: Apply once enrolled in college. Good fit for pre-vet students.

scholarships.name: AKC Companion Events Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Up to $10,000 (scholarship fund shared among recipients)
scholarships.eligibility: Students active in AKC Companion Events attending institution of higher education
scholarships.notes: Only applicable if Avery participates in AKC events (dog showing, obedience, etc.)

scholarships.name: EQUUS Foundation Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Junior and senior level undergraduate students enrolled in equine studies programs. Closely related majors (Animal Science with equine emphasis, Pre-Vet with equine focus) accepted.
scholarships.notes: For college juniors/seniors, not incoming freshmen. Bookmark for later.

scholarships.name: AQHA (American Quarter Horse Foundation) Scholarships
scholarships.amount: $2,250-$10,000 depending on specific scholarship (50+ scholarships awarded annually)
scholarships.eligibility: Based on academic achievement, financial need, and Quarter Horse involvement. GPA minimums range from 2.5-3.0 depending on specific scholarship. Must demonstrate involvement with the equine industry.
scholarships.notes: Only applicable if Avery has Quarter Horse / equine involvement. Multiple scholarships available under one application.

scholarships.name: National FFA Scholarship Program
scholarships.eligibility: Active FFA member, high school senior or current college student. Must plan to attend or be enrolled in accredited post-secondary program.
scholarships.notes: Only if Avery is an FFA member. One of the largest agriculture scholarship programs in the country.

scholarships.name: Farm Credit Foundation for Agricultural Advancement Scholarship
scholarships.sponsor: Farm Credit Foundation for Agricultural Advancement
scholarships.eligibility: High school seniors or current college students pursuing agriculture career. Must reside in Horizon Farm Credit's territory (DE, MD, PA, VA, WV, DC).
scholarships.notes: GEOGRAPHIC LIMITATION: Only for students in DE, MD, PA, VA, WV, DC. Avery as a Georgia resident would NOT qualify for this one.

scholarships.name: Georgia 4-H Scholarships
scholarships.deadlineMonth: Varies by specific scholarship (check Georgia 4-H website)
scholarships.eligibility: Must be a Georgia 4-H member. Various scholarships available for different interests including animal science and agriculture.
scholarships.notes: Only if Avery is a 4-H member. Contact Georgia 4-H county extension for current opportunities.

scholarships.name: Georgia HOPE Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Covers a portion of tuition at eligible Georgia public colleges/universities (amount varies by institution)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: No separate application — automatic based on GPA at graduation
scholarships.eligibility: Georgia resident, graduate from eligible Georgia high school, minimum 3.0 HOPE GPA. Must maintain 3.0 GPA in college.
scholarships.notes: Avery qualifies with 3.85 GPA. Applies automatically if attending a Georgia public university (like UGA). Track status at GAfutures.org.

scholarships.name: Georgia Zell Miller Scholarship
scholarships.amount: Covers FULL tuition at eligible Georgia public colleges/universities
scholarships.deadlineMonth: No separate application — automatic based on GPA + test scores at graduation
scholarships.eligibility: Georgia resident, minimum 3.70 high school GPA (as calculated by GSFC) AND minimum 1200 SAT (math + reading) or 25 ACT composite. Must maintain 3.30 college GPA.
scholarships.notes: STRONG FIT: Avery's 3.85 GPA and 27 ACT both meet the thresholds. If attending UGA, this could cover full tuition. Verify GPA calculation method with GSFC.

scholarships.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
scholarships.eligibility: Current high school senior (or equivalent), U.S. citizen/permanent resident, minimum 3.0 GPA, planning to attend accredited U.S. college/university. Based on leadership and service.
scholarships.notes: Apply in fall of senior year (Aug-Sep 2026). Highly competitive but Avery qualifies. Strong community service/leadership record important.

scholarships.name: Horatio Alger National Scholarship
scholarships.deadlineMonth: March (application open December 1 - March 1 for juniors)
scholarships.eligibility: High school juniors/seniors who have overcome significant adversity, demonstrate financial need, integrity, and perseverance. Must be committed to earning a bachelor's degree.
scholarships.notes: Need-based — specifically for students who have overcome adversity. Only applicable if Avery's circumstances fit this criteria.

scholarships.name: Elks Most Valuable Student Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Current high school senior, U.S. citizen. Based on scholarship, leadership, and financial need.
scholarships.notes: Apply in fall of senior year. No need to be affiliated with the Elks. 500 scholarships makes this less competitive than some national awards.

scholarships.name: Breakthrough Junior Challenge
scholarships.amount: $250,000 (plus $50,000 for a teacher and $100,000 for school science lab)
scholarships.eligibility: Students aged 13-18. Must create an original video (under 90 seconds) explaining a concept in life sciences, physics, or math.
scholarships.notes: Extremely competitive but free to enter. Avery could explain a veterinary science or biology concept. Can apply now as a junior.

scholarships.name: UNCF STEM Scholars Program
scholarships.amount: Up to $25,000 (tiered over undergraduate years; 75 awards)
scholarships.eligibility: Students aspiring to earn STEM degrees. Check UNCF eligibility requirements.
scholarships.notes: Only applicable if Avery meets UNCF eligibility criteria.

scholarships.name: Society of Women Engineers (SWE) Emerging First Year Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Female students entering first year of undergraduate study. Must plan to study ABET-accredited engineering, technology, or computing program. High school GPA above 3.5.
scholarships.notes: IMPORTANT LIMITATION: Requires ABET-accredited engineering/technology/computing major. Animal Science/Pre-Vet would NOT qualify unless the program is ABET-accredited. Likely not a fit.

scholarships.name: Scholarships360 No Essay Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Open to high school students who plan to attend college. No essay required — just complete the form.
scholarships.notes: Easy to apply. Low barrier, so worth submitting.

scholarships.name: Auburn Competitive Merit Scholarships (Presidential Excellence Award)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: December (complete admission file by December 1; test scores by January 12)
scholarships.eligibility: Automatic consideration for admitted freshmen with complete file by Dec 1. Based on GPA + standardized test score.
scholarships.notes: No separate application needed. With 3.85 GPA and 27 ACT, Avery may qualify for a smaller merit award but the Presidential level typically requires higher test scores. Consider retaking ACT.

scholarships.name: Auburn AUSOM Departmental Scholarships (College of Agriculture)
scholarships.eligibility: Accepted freshmen who complete the AUSOM scholarship application.
scholarships.notes: Must complete AUSOM application after being admitted.

scholarships.name: Texas A&M Academic Scholarships (automatic merit)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: December (December 1 priority deadline via ApplyTexas or Common App)
scholarships.eligibility: Automatic consideration based on admission application. Factors: GPA, leadership, community service, special talents, first-gen status.
scholarships.notes: Must also submit SRAR (Self-Reported Academic Record). Apply by Dec 1 for best consideration.

scholarships.name: Texas A&M Department of Animal Science Scholarships
scholarships.amount: Varies ($150,000+ awarded annually to undergrads across the department)
scholarships.eligibility: Apply through university scholarship application. Awards based on achievement aligned with donor intent.
scholarships.notes: No separate departmental application — handled through the main university scholarship app.

scholarships.name: Texas A&M Lechner Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: Incoming freshmen admitted in fall. Based on academic achievement, leadership, and community/extracurricular participation. Linked to Honors admission.
scholarships.notes: Requires Honors admission. Competitive — typically needs higher test scores.

scholarships.name: Georgia HOPE Scholarship (at UGA)
scholarships.eligibility: Georgia resident with 3.0+ HOPE GPA graduating from eligible GA high school.
scholarships.notes: Avery qualifies. Applied automatically.

scholarships.name: Georgia Zell Miller Scholarship (at UGA)
scholarships.eligibility: Georgia resident, 3.70+ GPA (GSFC calculation), 25+ ACT or 1200+ SAT.
scholarships.notes: STRONG FIT: Avery meets both GPA (3.85) and ACT (27) thresholds. This is essentially free tuition at UGA.

scholarships.name: UGA Foundation Fellowship
scholarships.amount: Full academic funding + travel-study stipends + research grants (UGA's top scholarship)
scholarships.eligibility: Minimum 3.7 GPA, minimum 1400 SAT or 31 ACT, exceptional extracurricular/leadership record. Interview required.
scholarships.notes: ACT threshold (31) is above Avery's current 27. Would need significant ACT improvement to be competitive. Extremely selective.

scholarships.name: UGA CAES (College of Agricultural & Environmental Sciences) Scholarships
scholarships.deadlineMonth: Check UGA OSFA for current deadlines
scholarships.eligibility: Students enrolled in CAES majors (Animal Science, Animal Health, etc.). Available each academic year.
scholarships.notes: Apply through UGA financial aid system once enrolled.

scholarships.name: CSU Green and Gold Scholarship (Non-Resident)
scholarships.eligibility: Non-resident freshmen with 3.2+ GPA admitted to full-time degree-seeking program.
scholarships.notes: Avery qualifies with 3.85 GPA as a Georgia (non-CO) resident. Automatic — no separate app needed.

scholarships.name: CSU College of Veterinary Medicine & Biomedical Sciences Scholarships
scholarships.deadlineMonth: Complete CSUSA (CSU Scholarship Application) — deadline varies
scholarships.eligibility: Undergrads in the College of Vet Med & Biomedical Sciences. Complete the CSUSA, then the supplemental CVMBS scholarship application.
scholarships.notes: Must complete both the general CSU scholarship app AND the college-specific supplemental application.

scholarships.name: CSU Vet Prep Scholarship
scholarships.eligibility: First-year students in the Vet Prep Program in the College of Vet Med & Biomedical Sciences with demonstrated financial need.
scholarships.notes: Specific to Vet Prep students. Must file FAFSA.

scholarships.name: Purdue University Merit Scholarships (automatic)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: November (November 1 priority deadline for admission + scholarship consideration)
scholarships.eligibility: Automatic consideration for freshmen with complete admission application by Nov 1.
scholarships.notes: Apply early for best consideration.

scholarships.name: Purdue Department of Animal Sciences Scholarships
scholarships.amount: Varies ($72,200 total awarded across department in 2025-26)
scholarships.eligibility: Complete the Purdue Departmental Scholarship Application. Minimum 2.70 GPA. Various named scholarships with specific criteria (swine industry interest, dairy, etc.).
scholarships.notes: Separate departmental application required. Also file FAFSA by Dec 15 for need-based scholarships.

scholarships.name: Purdue REACH Program (Poultry Focus)
scholarships.amount: Full tuition for 8 semesters + book stipends + paid internship + study abroad
scholarships.eligibility: 5 incoming freshmen pursuing animal sciences degrees with a focus on poultry.
scholarships.notes: Only if Avery has interest in the poultry industry. Full ride but very specific focus.

scholarships.name: NC State PACK ASSIST Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: New students may apply upon admission through PACK ASSIST portal. College of Agriculture & Life Sciences awards require extra materials/essays.
scholarships.notes: One portal for all NC State scholarships. Apply as soon as admitted.

scholarships.name: NC State Goodnight Scholars Program
scholarships.deadlineMonth: February (apply to NC State by Feb 15, FAFSA by March 1 to be invited)
scholarships.eligibility: NC residents only from low/middle-income families pursuing STEM or affiliated education majors. Invitation-based.
scholarships.notes: NC RESIDENTS ONLY — Avery as a Georgia resident would NOT qualify.

scholarships.name: NC State Park Scholarships
scholarships.amount: Full ride (tuition, fees, room, board, stipends, study abroad funding)
scholarships.eligibility: Exceptional incoming freshmen. Highly competitive. Based on scholarship, leadership, service, and character.
scholarships.notes: Extremely competitive — NC State's premier scholarship. Open to out-of-state students.

scholarships.name: UTK Volunteer Scholarship (automatic merit)
scholarships.amount: Varies by GPA and test score tier
scholarships.eligibility: Automatic consideration based on admission application, GPA, and test scores.
scholarships.notes: Apply early. Check UTK's specific GPA/ACT award tiers to estimate amount.

scholarships.name: UTK College of Agricultural Sciences & Natural Resources Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Students in CASNR majors including Animal Science.
scholarships.notes: Departmental scholarships available once enrolled.

scholarships.name: Virginia Tech General Scholarship Application (University-wide)
scholarships.amount: Varies — hundreds of scholarships available through one application
scholarships.eligibility: Complete the General Scholarship Application in Scholarship Central. Open to all admitted students.
scholarships.notes: One application matches you to many scholarships. Also file FAFSA for need-based awards.

scholarships.name: Virginia Tech School of Animal Sciences Departmental Scholarships
scholarships.eligibility: Students in animal sciences. Applied through Scholarship Central.
scholarships.notes: Matched automatically based on Scholarship Central profile.

scholarships.name: Ohio State University Merit Scholarships (automatic)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: November (apply by early action deadline for merit consideration)
scholarships.eligibility: Automatic consideration based on admission application.
scholarships.notes: Apply early action for best merit scholarship consideration.

scholarships.name: Ohio State Scholarship Universe (departmental + college scholarships)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: February (February 1 priority for FAFSA + Scholarship Universe profile)
scholarships.eligibility: Complete FAFSA and create Scholarship Universe profile. Animal Science-specific scholarships include Charles B. Boyles Memorial (OH/WV residents preferred) and others.
scholarships.notes: One profile matches you to internal and external scholarships.

scholarships.name: Clemson CAFLS Scholarships (College of Agriculture, Forestry and Life Sciences)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: Submit Scholarship Interest Form prior to admission (rolling)
scholarships.eligibility: Incoming and transfer students are encouraged to complete the CAFLS Scholarship Interest Form prior to admission. Awards made by CAFLS Scholarships and Awards Committee.
scholarships.notes: Submit interest form early — before or at time of admission application.

scholarships.name: Clemson National Scholars Program
scholarships.amount: Full cost of attendance (tuition, fees, housing, food, etc.)
scholarships.deadlineMonth: Requires Honors College application (check Clemson Honors for deadline)
scholarships.eligibility: Premier academic recruitment scholarship. Requires Honors College application and interview.
scholarships.notes: Extremely competitive. Typically requires very high test scores.

applyNowAsJunior: Scholarships360 No Essay Scholarship — deadline June 30, 2026
Horatio Alger Scholarship — Dec 1 to March 1 (if applicable)
applySeniorYearFall2026: School admission applications (all trigger automatic merit consideration) — Nov 1 to Dec 1 depending on school
National FFA Scholarships — January 15, 2027 (if FFA member)
Virginia Tech General Scholarship App — January 22, 2027
applyOnceEnrolledFreshmanYear: National Dairy Shrine Maurice E. Core — April 15
Purdue Animal Sciences Departmental (if at Purdue)
actionPlan.keyTip: The Georgia Zell Miller Scholarship is Avery's single highest-value opportunity if attending UGA — it covers full tuition with NO separate application. Avery already meets both the GPA (3.85 vs 3.70 required) and ACT (27 vs 25 required) thresholds.
